In an opinion rendered this morn
ing for the people fighting the
north hill sewer grab, Judge VIM.
E. Richardson flatly disagrees with
the opinion given yesterday by
Corporation Counsel Morrill,, to the
effect that there was no appeal to
the referendum against an assess
ment ordinance.


Judge Richardson holds that un,
der the present city charter there
Is an appeal to the referendum, and
that no decision of the supreme
court or act of the legislature ex-
lists that cuts 1t off.


In a previous opinion Judge Rich
ardson held that while the appeal
to the referendum was permitted
under the present charter it is cut
off under the proposed new charter
which permits of no appeal of this
kind from an Improvement or as
sessment ordinance.


The opinion rendered today by
Judge Richardson reads:
